What happened this week:
  - Stu's Crew added Miller, Kendre (RB, NOS) (free agent).
  - Weichert's Warmongers won McPherson, Evan (PK, CIN) on waivers.

--- SHAREABLE RECAP ---
The Playmakers put up 192.15 behind Bo Nix's season-high 46.75 points, demolishing Steve 192.15-92.65 in the week's most lopsided result. KP's explosion came just one week after scoring 85.70 — a 106-point swing that buried the Warmongers by nearly 100 points. Steve left 60.70 points on his bench, including Justin Herbert's 38.10, but even optimal lineups wouldn't have touched the Playmakers' eruption.

Miller extended his win streak to five games, edging Brandon 116.25-109.20 in the week's tightest finish. Brandon actually showed life for once, jumping from 79.80 to 109.20 behind Drake Maye's 29.30, but still sits at 0-7. The margin was just 7.05 points — closer than any of Brandon's previous six losses this season.

Stu beat Ben 144.90-132.90 despite Ben leaving 68.70 points on his bench, led by Matthew Stafford's unused 39.20. Ben's been carrying multiple quarterbacks all season and it cost him again — Stafford outscored his starter by 37 points. Pat topped Michele 127.65-115.10 with Keenan Allen continuing to pay off his $21 FAAB investment, while Eddie extended his own win streak to two games, beating Robb 108.10-87.25.

The top of the standings tightened with three teams now at 6-1. KP leads on points scored at 978.2, followed by Miller at 830.3 and Pat at 816.8. Michele dropped to 4-3 after his first loss, while Brandon's winless streak reached seven games to start the season.
--- END SHAREABLE RECAP ---
